title: 💻 Claude Code power user tips
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

This article collects workflow tips from the Claude Code team at Anthropic. These practices cover parallel execution, planning, automation, verification, and customization—the patterns the team uses every day to ship code faster. Everyone’s setup is different, so experiment to see what works for you. **Important:** The single most impactful tip in this guide is **verification**—giving Claude a way to check its own output. These are power-user patterns collected from individual engineers on the Claude Code team. * Most commands shown here ship with Claude Code: /color and /btw are **built-in commands**, and /simplify and /loop are **bundled skills** that ship with the CLI. See the **commands reference** and **skills**. You can build your own skills by adding a SKILL.md file under .claude/skills//. Community plugins (for example the “ralph-wiggum” plugin) are not reviewed or sanctioned by Anthropic — check with your administrator before installing third-party plugins in a managed environment. Everything else in this guide works on a stock Claude Code install.

title: 💻 Anthropic Just Revealed The Best Claude Code Setup
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Anthropic Just Revealed The Best Claude Code Setup
AI LABS
142000 subscribers
510 likes
22375 views
21 May 2026
The best Claude Code setup isn't the model, it's the harness. This Claude Code tutorial covers the full setup plus the Claude Code tips that stop agents failing at scale.
Try CleanMyMac 7 days FREE and use our code AILABS for 20% off - https://clnmy.com/AILABS


Community with All Resources 📦: http://ailabspro.io
Video Code: V60

We're building The Roundup — a daily newsletter covering the AI stories that actually matter.  
Join now at: https://www.theroundup.so/

Article: https://claude.com/blog/how-claude-code-works-in-large-codebases-best-practices-and-where-to-start


Why agents break on large codebases and how to fix it. The best Claude Code setup is about the harness, not the model. We walk through how coding agents navigate code, why RAG-based retrieval falls apart at scale, and the Claude Code tips that keep agents working as your project grows.

How the agent finds your code:
- Why RAG-based retrieval causes agents to hallucinate modules that no longer exist
- How file-system navigation with bash tools keeps the context window clean
- Why the model alone doesn't decide your output, the harness does

The harness matters more than the model:
- The real answer to the codex vs claude code debate: a strong model with a weak harness is wasted
- How to use Claude Code with a harness tailored to your own project, not just the defaults
- Where open-source harnesses help and where you need to build your own

The 7 pieces of a Claude Code harness:
- CLAUDE.md done right, kept around 300 lines, with a separate file per subdirectory in a monorepo
- Hooks: session-start, pre-tool-use, and the stop hook that updates CLAUDE.md with what it learned
- Claude Code skills with progressive disclosure, scoped to paths so they only load when needed
- Plugins that bundle skills, hooks and MCPs to share the same context across a team
- LSP so the agent navigates code like a developer, critical for unconventional languages
- MCPs that connect the agent to your internal tools, data and APIs
- Subagents with isolated context windows for parallel work without bloating the main agent

Large-codebase practices:
- Separate tests per subdirectory to avoid timeouts
- Build a codebase map for unconventional languages like C++
- Use ignore files so the agent leaves the wrong files alone
- Review and prune your setup as models evolve, because instructions for one model waste tokens on the next

Same principles, other workflows:
- The same setup applies whether you use Claude Code free or on a paid plan
- These harness principles carry over to creative and tooling workflows, like wiring Claude Code into Remotion for video editing or Obsidian for notes
- How to use Claude Code for free or paid, the harness is what changes your results either way
- A note for our Claude Code gratis viewers: the workflow is the same no matter how you access the model

This is practical AI automation for builders: real ai tools, real ai agents, and the kind of vibe coding setup that actually holds up when a project gets serious. Built on Anthropic's own guidance for scaling Claude AI agents.
00:00 Intro
00:32 How agents navigate code (RAG vs file system)
01:46 Why your harness matters more than the model
02:48 Structuring claude.md at scale
04:19 Sponsor
05:09 Hooks
06:23 Skills
07:25 Plugins
08:42 LSP for deeper code navigation
09:57 Custom MCPs for internal tools
11:04 Sub-agents and parallel delegation
12:26 Best practices for large codebases

title: 💻 Claude Code /batch vs claude -p: Parallel Changes or CI ...
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

## Conclusion¶

Claude Code's batch processing is built on two pillars: `/batch` and headless mode (`claude -p`).

`/batch` enables safe parallel execution of framework migrations and naming convention unification through Git Worktree agent isolation and automatic PR creation. Headless mode handles CI/CD pipeline and shell script integration, serving as the foundation for embedding AI reviews and automated analysis into existing workflows. [...] Choose `/batch` when applying bulk changes across an entire project. Choose headless mode when embedding AI processing into existing CI/CD pipelines.

## /batch Command: Parallel Execution of Large-Scale Code Changes¶

### Basic Usage¶

`/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. Simply describe the desired change in natural language, and it handles codebase investigation, implementation, and PR creation automatically. [...] The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. It runs up to 10x faster than sending individual prompts and integrates directly into CI/CD pipelines.

Key Points

It provides a strategic framework for selecting the optimal execution method based on scale: utilizing agent swarms for rapid parallel processing of 5-20 items, the Anthropic API Batch for 50% cost savings on massive datasets, or sequential loops for tasks with complex dependencies. title: 💻 What Is Claude Code /simplify and /batch? The Built-In Commands That Fix Overengineering | MindStudio
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Claude Code is Anthropic’s terminal-based AI coding assistant. Unlike chat-based tools, it operates directly in your development environment — reading files, running commands, editing code, and executing terminal operations as part of a continuous workflow. [...] `/simplify` analyzes code and returns a concrete, simpler version — most useful after prototyping, during code review, or when working in unfamiliar parts of a codebase.
 `/batch` runs Claude Code operations in parallel across multiple files — best for large migrations, pattern enforcement, and any change that needs to apply consistently at scale. [...] `/batch` is designed for situations where you need to apply the same operation across many files or make multiple related changes simultaneously. Instead of running the same prompt file by file, you describe what you want done, tell Claude Code which files to target, and it handles execution in parallel.

This matters when you’re doing things like:

title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

If you're new to Claude Code, see the getting started guide first. If you need something bigger -- migrating an entire codebase from one framework to another, or applying a change across dozens of files -- that's what `/batch` is for. These two commands shipped in Claude Code v2.1.63 and they cover two workflows that used to require careful multi-step prompting.

## What Claude Code /simplify Does [...] `/simplify` and `/batch` are bundled Claude Code commands -- they ship with every installation and work out of the box. Custom slash commands, by contrast, are project-specific prompts you write yourself in your CLAUDE.md configuration or `.claude/commands/` directory, like skills you define for your workflow. Bundled commands are maintained by Anthropic and receive updates with each Claude Code release. [...] `/simplify` and `/batch` signal where Claude Code is headed: bundled multi-agent workflows that handle common engineering patterns out of the box. You still review every diff and approve every plan. But the orchestration -- spinning up parallel agents, managing worktrees, aggregating results -- is no longer something you build yourself. Expect more bundled commands like these as the platform matures.

title: 💻 Claude Code: s2-streamstore/claude-batch-toolkit: Send non-urgent work ...
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

MCP Server (`claude_batch_mcp.py`): Python script run by `uv`. Exposes `send_to_batch`, `batch_status`, `batch_fetch`, `batch_list`, `batch_poll_once` tools. Also works as a CLI.
 Skill (`SKILL.md`): Teaches Claude Code how and when to use the batch tools. Invoked with `/batchy`. Loaded automatically.
 Status Line (`statusline.sh`): Bash script that renders batch job counts in the Claude Code status bar and triggers background polling via `curl`+`jq`. [...] ## Why `/batchy`?

The skill was originally named `/batch`, but Claude Code introduced a built-in `/batch` command with different semantics (#4). To avoid the collision, we renamed to `/batchy`. Existing installs are migrated automatically when you re-run `./install.sh`.

## Usage

### Submit work to batch

In Claude Code, just say:

```
/batchy Review this codebase for security issues 
```

```
/batchy Generate comprehensive tests for src/auth/ 
``` [...] ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Claude Code Session │ │ │ │ User: "/batchy review src/ for security issues" │ │ │ │ Claude: │ │ 1. Reads all files in src/ │ │ 2. Assembles self-contained prompt (bash → temp file) │ │ 3. Calls send_to_batch MCP tool with packet_path │ │ 4. Reports: "Submitted job msgbatch_abc123" │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Status Bar │ │ │ │ [Opus] 42% | $1.23 | batch: 1 pending │ │ │

title: 💻 Claude Code: Medium
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Building Custom Skills in Claude Code: A Simple Guide. Automate your development workflows with custom Claude Code skills. Claude Code is revolutionizing how developers interact with AI-powered coding assistants. One of its most powerful features is the ability to create **custom skills** — reusable, automated workflows that can handle complex, multi-step tasks with a single command. > In this guide, we’ll walk through creating a real-world skill that automates the entire process of initializing a git repository, authenticating with GitHub, creating a remote repository, and syncing everything — all with cross-platform support. ## Step 1: Installing Claude Code. Before we dive into creating skills, you’ll need to have Claude Code installed on your system. ## Step 2: Understanding the Skills Architecture. Claude Code skills are stored in a specific directory structure within your user configuration. Let’s explore where these skills live and how they’re organized. Data scientist with a passion for AI, Regularly blogging about LLM and OpenAI's innovations,Sharing insights for AI community growth.
